summary: A brief link-blog post from 2005 where Shapiro shares plans he found (via Boing Boing) for making baby wipes using a table saw. The post is essentially a one-liner celebrating the absurd ingenuity of the project. It reflects Shapiro's early blogging style and his long-standing appreciation for creative maker projects.
key_points: Highlights a DIY project that repurposes a table saw to cut baby wipes
Curated via Boing Boing, reflecting the early-2000s link-blogging culture
Demonstrates Shapiro's affinity for inventive, unconventional maker projects
money_quotes: What can I say… it's plans to make baby wipes with your table saw. How cool is that.
key_insight: Even the most mundane household problems can inspire creative maker solutions with unexpected tools.
